children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: There is established the United States Travel and Tourism Advisory Board (referred to in this section as the “Board”), the members of which shall be appointed by the Secretary of Commerce for 2-year terms from companies and organizations in the travel and tourism industry.

children.heading: Executive Director
children.label: b Executive Director
children.text: The Assistant Secretary of Commerce for Travel and Tourism shall serve as the Executive Director of the Board.

children.heading: Executive Secretariat
children.label: c Executive Secretariat
children.text: The National Travel and Tourism Office of the International Trade Administration shall serve as the Executive Secretariat for the Board.
serve as the advisory body to the Secretary of Commerce on matters relating to the travel and tourism industry in the United States;
advise the Secretary of Commerce on government policies and programs that affect the United States travel and tourism industry;
offer counsel on current and emerging issues;
provide a forum for discussing and proposing solutions to problems related to the travel and tourism industry; and
provide advice regarding the domestic travel and tourism industry as an economic engine.

children.heading: Recovery strategies
children.label: e Recovery strategies
children.text: The Board shall assist the Assistant Secretary of Commerce for Travel and Tourism in the development and implementation of the recovery strategies required under .section 9803(e)(1) of this title
